summary: MAUI app crashes on Windows IoT Enterprise (and systems with old GPU drivers) when instantiating SKCanvasView, due to REGDB_E_CLASSNOTREG failure while activating the WinRT class SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.BufferExtensions — caused by missing MSVC C++ runtime on the target machine.

bugSignals.errorMessage: The type initializer for 'SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.BufferExtensions' threw an exception. Inner: COMException Class not registered (0x80040154 REGDB_E_CLASSNOTREG)
bugSignals.stackTrace: BufferExtensions..cctor() -> WinRT.BaseActivationFactory..ctor -> REGDB_E_CLASSNOTREG in SkiaSharp_Views_WinUI_Native_Projection; SKXamlCanvas.CreateBitmap() -> SKXamlCanvas.DoInvalidate()

stepsToReproduce: Create a .NET MAUI 8 application targeting Windows (WinUI)
Run on Windows IoT Enterprise or a system without MSVC C++ 2015+ Redistributable
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Windows IoT Enterprise / old Ryzen+Radeon GPU systems; SkiaSharp 3.116.0–3.116.1 and 2.88.9; .NET MAUI 8.0

repoLinks.description: Maintainer-created repro test app to isolate COM crash

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Maintainer confirmed the fix (static MSVC runtime linking) was pending pipeline signing issues as of August 2025 but no release has addressed it yet; April 2026 follow-up confirms still unfixed.

analysis.summary: The WinRT native component SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll fails to activate its BufferExtensions class on Windows IoT Enterprise and certain GPU driver environments. The C# WinRT projection (SkiaSharp_Views_WinUI_Native_Projection) calls RoActivateInstance to resolve IBufferExtensionsStatics, but this fails with REGDB_E_CLASSNOTREG because the DLL cannot load (missing MSVC C++ runtime). The maintainer identified the root cause as a build issue: the native DLL was not statically linking the MSVC runtime. The fix is to compile SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll with /MT (static CRT) so it has no external MSVC runtime dependency.
analysis.rationale: Crash is confirmed with a full WinDbg dump, stack trace points directly to the WinRT activation factory for BufferExtensions in the native projection DLL. The maintainer (@mattleibow) explicitly confirmed this is a known issue caused by not statically linking the runtime, and said a fix was in progress. Affected platforms limited to Windows IoT / minimal Windows installations. Workaround (install VC++ redistributable) confirmed effective by community. Classified as high severity because it is a hard crash with no SkiaSharp-level workaround on systems users don't control.

keySignals.source: WinDbg crash dump in issue body
keySignals.interpretation: WinRT activation of the native component fails — DLL cannot be loaded/registered, most likely due to missing VC++ runtime

keySignals.text: I think that I was building the interop and not statically linking the runtime. Try installing the msvc runtime. That fixed it for my tests.
keySignals.interpretation: Root cause confirmed: shipped binaries were built without static MSVC runtime linkage; fix is known

keySignals.text: I found out that the system with old Ryzen / Radeon GPUs has the same problem with this regardless of the Windows edition.
keySignals.interpretation: Issue is not strictly limited to IoT edition — also affects systems with outdated GPU drivers that may ship without VC++ runtime

keySignals.text: For those affected, it seems to be fixed by manually installing VC as @mattleibow suggested above. winget install Microsoft.VCRedist.2015+.x64
keySignals.interpretation: Workaround confirmed effective — validates that missing MSVC runtime is the root cause

codeInvestigation.finding: vcxproj shows Release config uses RuntimeLibrary=MultiThreaded (/MT — static CRT). This is correct for static linking. However, maintainer statement suggests historically shipped binaries may have been built differently (dynamic CRT). The current project file shows the intended fix configuration.

GetPixels() on WriteableBitmap calls PixelBuffer.GetByteBuffer() which calls BufferExtensions.GetByteBuffer(buffer) — the WinRT static method that triggers activation of SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.BufferExtensions. This is the call site that crashes.

BufferExtensions is declared as a WinRT runtimeclass with a static method GetByteBuffer. As a WinRT runtimeclass, it requires activation factory registration — explaining why REGDB_E_CLASSNOTREG can occur if the DLL fails to load.

CreateBitmap() creates a WriteableBitmap and calls bitmap.GetPixels() on line 240. This call triggers the BufferExtensions WinRT activation. The crash occurs on the first paint/invalidation cycle, matching the reported behavior of crashing immediately on SKCanvasView instantiation.

workarounds: Install Microsoft Visual C++ 2015-2022 Redistributable (x64): winget install Microsoft.VCRedist.2015+.x64
Include VC++ runtime installer in your application's deployment package/installer so end users don't need to install it manually
nextQuestions: Has the fix (static MSVC runtime linking) been applied to the build pipeline and released in any version since August 2025?
Does the issue also affect ARM64 builds of the WinUI native component?
Is the crash reproducible with the latest preview builds of SkiaSharp?

resolution.hypothesis: The released SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll was compiled with dynamic MSVC runtime linkage (/MD), causing it to depend on VCRUNTIME140.dll/MSVCP140.dll which are absent on Windows IoT Enterprise and minimal Windows installations. Building with /MT (static CRT) removes this dependency.

proposals.title: Include VC++ Redistributable in app deployment
proposals.description: Package the Microsoft Visual C++ 2015–2022 Redistributable with your application installer so end users on Windows IoT or stripped-down Windows environments have the runtime present.

proposals.title: Fix SkiaSharp build to statically link MSVC runtime
proposals.description: Ensure SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.vcxproj consistently uses RuntimeLibrary=MultiThreaded (/MT) in all build configurations including the CI/CD pipeline so shipped binaries have no dynamic CRT dependency. Maintainer has confirmed this is the planned fix.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Fix SkiaSharp build to statically link MSVC runtime
resolution.recommendedReason: This is the correct permanent fix identified by the maintainer. The workaround requires users to modify their deployment which is not always possible (e.g., locked-down IoT environments).

actionability.reason: Root cause is identified (missing static MSVC runtime in shipped binaries) and maintainer confirmed a fix was planned. However, as of April 2026 the fix has not been released. Issue needs tracking to confirm whether a fixed build has been published or if the pipeline signing issues are still blocking.

missingInfo: Which SkiaSharp version (if any) contains the fix with statically-linked MSVC runtime?
Is the pipeline signing issue that was blocking the fix now resolved?

actions.description: Apply area, platform, tenet, and partner labels

actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed WinDbg analysis. The root cause is that the `SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll` was shipped without statically linking the MSVC C++ runtime, so it fails to load on systems without the Visual C++ Redistributable installed (which includes Windows IoT Enterprise and some OEM systems with older GPU drivers).

**Immediate workaround:** Install the Microsoft Visual C++ 2015–2022 Redistributable on the target machine:
```
winget install Microsoft.VCRedist.2015+.x64
```

Alternatively, include the VC++ runtime installer in your application's deployment package.

A fix (statically linking the runtime in the build pipeline) is in progress. We'll update this issue when a fixed release is available.
